mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate lowers discount rates and eases financial conditions, which lifts equity valuations and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal is strongest when inflation is stable or declining, because rate cuts are then read as liquidity support rather than recession panic.
failure_condition: Reject if 20d forward SPX returns are not higher after negative 3-month changes in FEDFUNDS, or if the effect flips negative during stable CPI periods in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises sharply, markets price slower earnings growth and higher recession risk, so S&P 500 forward returns turn negative over the next month. The effect should be clearest after a sustained deterioration in labor conditions rather than a one-off monthly noise print.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes do not produce below-baseline 20d SPX returns, or if positive returns occur in more than 60% of instances after the labor deterioration threshold is met.

mechanism_narrative: A concurrent rise in RSAFS and HOUST indicates improving household and business demand, which supports revenue expectations and equity prices over a quarterly horizon. The mechanism fails when higher sales are driven only by inflation or when housing weakens despite higher retail activity.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint increase in RSAFS and HOUST does not outperform either series alone for predicting positive 60d SPX returns, or if the interaction term is not positive in rolling out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A surge in INDPRO signals stronger real output and factory activity, which boosts cyclical earnings expectations and lifts S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal is strongest when labor and consumption data are not simultaneously weakening, because then the production gain reflects genuine expansion rather than inventory noise.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO increases by at least 0.4% over 2 months
failure_condition: Reject if high-INDPRO episodes fail to produce positive 20d SPX returns after conditioning on non-declining PAYEMS and RSAFS, or if the signal becomes negative in out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in CPIAUCSL while FEDFUNDS remains elevated signals disinflation under restrictive policy, which raises the probability of future easing and expands equity multiples. This should generate positive S&P 500 returns over the next week because markets reprice rates quickly.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change falls below 2% while FEDFUNDS stays above 4%
failure_condition: Reject if triggered disinflation episodes do not deliver positive 5d SPX returns more often than not, or if returns are indistinguishable from baseline after controlling for FEDFUNDS.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent payroll growth in PAYEMS supports consumption, margins, and earnings revisions, so S&P 500 returns should be positive over a 20-day horizon. The signal should weaken if unemployment is simultaneously rising, because payroll gains then reflect labor-market lag rather than durable demand.
failure_condition: Reject if positive payroll momentum does not improve 20d SPX returns relative to baseline, or if the effect disappears when UNRATE is flat to lower.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in FEDFUNDS above a restrictive threshold suppresses valuation multiples and tightens credit conditions, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. The effect is strongest when inflation has not yet fallen materially, because real rates then rise and policy remains contractionary.
failure_condition: Reject if periods with FEDFUNDS above 5% do not show below-baseline 20d SPX returns, or if the sign becomes positive when CPIAUCSL is still accelerating.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rebound in GDPC1 and RSAFS identifies broad real demand acceleration, which should lift expected earnings and drive positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter. The mechanism fails when GDP is revised away from growth or retail sales rise only from price effects rather than real volume demand.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 is positive q/q and RSAFS rises for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: Reject if the joint GDP-sales expansion does not predict positive 60d SPX returns after adjusting for CPIAUCSL, or if revised GDPC1 removes the signal in retrospective validation.
